ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for iced low-fat caramel macchiato. FOODON_03301710 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (Coffee-based beverages) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for iced low-fat caramel macchiato variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 (Coffee drink) is the closest available parent concept; no specific code for caramel macchiato variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall (12 fl oz / 354 ml) serving of iced low-fat caramel macchiato, weighing approximately 370 g including ice and liquid.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato with Non-fat Milk — Nutrition Facts (Tall, 12 fl oz)
nutrient_source.organization: Starbucks Corporation / USDA Branded Food (FDC ID reference) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato (non-fat/low-fat milk, tall size) and cross-referenced with Starbucks published nutrition data. Values scaled to per-100g basis from a 370g tall serving. The 'less sweet' modifier reduces vanilla syrup pumps by approximately one pump, resulting in a modest reduction in sugar (~2–3 g per serving); per-100g values adjusted accordingly. Low-fat milk (1% fat) used in place of non-fat milk adds marginal fat contribution.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — milk-based sweetened coffee beverages; lactose and sucrose GI reference values
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced low-fat caramel macchiato. GI estimated at 55 based on analogous milk-based sweetened coffee beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database. Low-fat milk has a GI of approximately 30–34; added sugars (vanilla syrup, caramel sauce) elevate the blended GI. A blended GI of 55 (low) is defensible for a sweetened milk-coffee drink with approximately 34 g carbohydrate per 370 g serving. GL per serving = (55 × 34.0) / 100 = 18.7, rounded to 18.8.

hypertension.guidance: Each tall serving provides approximately 167 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should account for this within their daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and note that caffeine from espresso may transiently raise blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars from vanilla syrup and caramel drizzle elevating glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of ~18.8 per tall serving, this beverage can cause a meaningful postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption or request further sugar reduction and monitor blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat due to low-fat milk; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The use of low-fat milk limits saturated fat to approximately 1.9 g per serving, which is within acceptable limits for cardiovascular health; however, the added sugars from syrup and caramel drizzle contribute to total caloric intake and should be moderated in a heart-healthy diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Low-Fat Caramel Macchiato (Less Sweet) is a chilled espresso-based coffee beverage served at Starbucks Singapore. It is prepared by combining low-fat (1%) milk with vanilla-flavoured syrup, pouring the mixture over ice, marking it with a shot of espresso, and finishing with a caramel sauce drizzle on top. The 'less sweet' customisation reduces the number of vanilla syrup pumps compared to the standard recipe, resulting in a lighter sweetness profile while retaining the characteristic caramel and espresso flavour. The drink has a creamy, milky texture with a pronounced coffee bitterness balanced by sweet caramel and vanilla notes. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 54 kcal, 2.7 g protein, 0.8 g fat, and 9.2 g carbohydrate, with 8.5 g sugar. The beverage has a low glycemic index of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 18.8 per tall serving, making it a moderate glycemic beverage. It is a source of calcium (~90 mg/100 g) and vitamin B12 from the low-fat milk component. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious due to the added sugars from syrup and caramel drizzle; those with hypertension should note the sodium and caffeine content. The beverage is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is commonly ordered as an afternoon coffee treat or a lighter alternative to full-fat espresso drinks in Singapore's café culture.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Starbucks iced caramel macchiato (low-fat/non-fat milk, tall size) and scaled to per-100g. The 'less sweet' modifier is a Starbucks customisation not separately listed in any approved nutrient database; sugar values are adjusted by estimation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar milk-based sweetened coffee beverages. No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product variant.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are scaled from a 370 g tall (12 fl oz) serving to per-100g basis. The 'less sweet' customisation reduces vanilla syrup by approximately one pump (~5 g syrup, ~4 g sugar reduction per serving), reflected in the sugar_g estimate. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA Branded Food data for this product category; low-fat dairy and coffee syrups do not contribute measurable trans fat. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a liquid milk-coffee beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee and milk beverages do not contain meaningful vitamin C. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 7. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 9. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 10. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 11. GI set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for milk-based sweetened coffee beverages; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 12. Multilingual names provided for Singapore context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-served beverage. 13. This is a Starbucks Singapore menu item served at Starbucks One Fullerton per field survey source data.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified need to verify all aliases_by_language entries appear in flat aliases list and correct GI category.

Iteration 2: Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' as GI 55 falls into the low GI category per University of Sydney GI Database definitions. Updated related text in `gi_source.source_match_notes`, `health_context.type2_diabetes.guidance`, and `llm_training.natural_language_description` to reflect 'low' GI. Verified aliases consistency: identified and added the romanized alias 'Ais Rendah Lemak Karamel Macchiato (Kurang Manis)' from `aliases_by_language.romanized` to the flat `aliases` array to ensure perfect consistency. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed zero-value fields (trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg) documented with justification phrases. Confirmed GI is numeric >0 with gi_category set. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
